docs.title: GGE: A Standardized Framework for Evaluating Gene Expression Generative Models
docs.text:
Paper: Accepted at the Gen2 Workshop at ICLR 2026

Comprehensive, standardized evaluation of generated gene expression data.

GGE (Generated Genetic Expression Evaluator) addresses the urgent need for standardized evaluation in single-cell gene expression generative models. Current practices suffer from inconsistent metric implementations, incomparable hyperparameter choices, and lack of biologically-grounded metrics. GGE provides:

  • Comprehensive suite of distributional metrics with explicit computation space options
  • Biologically-motivated evaluation through DEG-focused analysis with perturbation-effect correlation
  • Standardized reporting for reproducible benchmarking

Full Documentation with API tutorials can be found here

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Per-metric space configuration (raw, PCA, DEG)
  • Perturbation-effect correlation (Paper Eq. 1)
  • Configurable DEG thresholds
  • GPU (CUDA) and Apple MPS acceleration
  • Per-gene and aggregate metrics
  • Publication-quality visualizations (static and interactive)
  • Simple Python API and CLI
  • Mixed-space evaluation with evaluate_lazy()

docs.title: Metrics
docs.text:
All metrics are computed per-gene (returning a vector) and aggregated:

Metric Description Direction Pearson Correlation Linear correlation between expression profiles Higher is better Spearman Correlation Rank correlation (robust to outliers) Higher is better R² Coefficient of determination Higher is better Perturbation-Effect Correlation Correlation on (real - ctrl) vs (gen - ctrl) Higher is better MSE Mean Squared Error Lower is better Wasserstein-1 Earth Mover's Distance (L1) Lower is better Wasserstein-2 Sinkhorn-regularized OT Lower is better MMD Maximum Mean Discrepancy (RBF kernel) Lower is better Energy Distance Statistical potential energy Lower is better

docs.title: Visualizations
docs.text:
• Boxplots and violin plots for metric distributions
  • Radar plots for multi-metric comparison
  • Scatter plots for real vs generated expression
  • Embedding plots (PCA/UMAP) for real vs generated data
  • Heatmaps for per-gene metric values
  • Interactive Plotly plots with density overlays and metadata coloring

docs.title: Computation Spaces
docs.text:
GGE treats computation space as a first-class parameter (see Paper Section 3.3):

Space Description When to Use Raw Gene Space Full ~5,000–20,000 gene dimensions Gene-level interpretability needed PCA Space Reduced k-dimensional space (default: 50) Primary distributional metrics DEG Space Restricted to differentially expressed genes Biologically-targeted evaluation

Recommendation: Use multi-space evaluation—PCA-50 for distributional metrics, DEG for biological focus.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
pip install gge-eval

The package includes GPU-accelerated metrics via geomloss, which automatically falls back to CPU if no GPU is available.

docs.title: Python API
docs.text:
from gge import evaluate

# From file paths
results = evaluate(
    real_data="real_data.h5ad",
    generated_data="generated_data.h5ad",
    condition_columns=["perturbation", "cell_type"],
    split_column="split",  # Optional: for train/test
    output_dir="evaluation_output/"
)

# From AnnData objects
import scanpy as sc
real_adata = sc.read_h5ad("real_data.h5ad")
generated_adata = sc.read_h5ad("generated_data.h5ad")

results = evaluate(
    real_data=real_adata,
    generated_data=generated_adata,
    condition_columns=["perturbation"],
)

# Access results
print(results.summary())

# Get metric for specific split
test_results = results.get_split("test")
for condition, cond_result in test_results.conditions.items():
    print(f"{condition}: Pearson={cond_result.get_metric_value('pearson'):.3f}")

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Contributions are welcome! Please feel free to submit a pull request or open an issue.

docs.title: Citation
docs.text:
If you use GGE in your research, please cite our paper:

@misc{rubbi2026gge,
  title  = {A Standardized Framework for Evaluating Gene Expression Generative Models},
  author = {Rubbi, Andrea and Di Francesco, Andrea Giuseppe and Lotfollahi, Mohammad and Liò, Pietro},
  year   = {2026},
  note   = {Presented at the GenAI in Genomics Workshop at ICLR 2026},
  url    = {https://genai-in-genomics.github.io/}
}

or the software

@software{rubbi2026gge,
  author = {Rubbi, Andrea},
  title  = {GGE: Generated Genetic Expression Evaluator},
  year   = {2026},
  url    = {https://github.com/AndreaRubbi/GGE}
}

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: This page provides detailed API documentation for GGE's main functions and classes.

docs.title: gge.evaluate(real_data, generated_data, condition_columns, split_column=None, output_dir=None, metrics=None, include_multivariate=False, control_key=None, control_column=None, verbose=True, **loader_kwargs)
docs.text:
Convenience function to run full evaluation.

All metrics support computation in different spaces (raw, pca, deg) through their space parameter.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: real_data : str, Path, or AnnData Path to real data h5ad file or AnnData object generated_data : str, Path, or AnnData Path to generated data h5ad file or AnnData object condition_columns : List[str] Columns to match between datasets split_column : str, optional Column indicating train/test split output_dir : str or Path, optional Directory to save results metrics : List, optional Metrics to compute include_multivariate : bool Whether to include multivariate metrics verbose : bool Print progress **loader_kwargs Additional arguments for data loader

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: EvaluationResult Complete evaluation results

docs.title: From paths
docs.text: results = evaluate( ... "real.h5ad", ... "generated.h5ad", ... condition_columns=["perturbation", "cell_type"], ... output_dir="evaluation_output/" ... )

docs.title: From AnnData objects
docs.text: results = evaluate( ... real_adata, ... generated_adata, ... condition_columns=["perturbation"], ... )

docs.title: Mixed (path + AnnData)
docs.text: results = evaluate( ... "real.h5ad", ... generated_adata, ... condition_columns=["perturbation"], ... )

docs.title: gge.evaluate_lazy(real_path, generated_path, condition_columns, metrics, control_key=None, control_column=None, split_column=None, output_dir=None, verbose=True, **loader_kwargs)
docs.text:
Lazy-loading evaluation function for gene expression data.

This function provides a streamlined API for evaluating generative models with explicit metric configuration. Each metric can specify its own space (raw, pca, or deg) for computation.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default real_path str

Path to real gene expression data

required generated_path str

Path to generated gene expression data

required condition_columns Union[str, List[str]]

Column(s) for condition-wise stratification (string or list)

required metrics List[BaseMetric]

List of metric instances with their space configurations

required control_key Optional[str]

Value identifying control samples for DEG space (required if any metric uses "deg" space)

None control_column Optional[str]

Column containing control identifier (defaults to first condition column)

None split_column Optional[str]

Optional column for train/test split evaluation

None output_dir Optional[str]

Optional directory to save evaluation results

None verbose bool

Whether to show progress information

True **loader_kwargs

Additional arguments passed to data loader

{}

Returns:

Type Description EvaluationResult

EvaluationResult object containing computed metrics

Example

from gge import evaluate_lazy from gge.metrics import PearsonCorrelation, Wasserstein2Distance, MMDDistance

metrics = [ ... PearsonCorrelation(space="deg", deg_lfc=0.25, deg_pval=0.1), ... Wasserstein2Distance(space="pca", n_components=50), ... MMDDistance(space="pca", n_components=50), ... ]

results = evaluate_lazy( ... "real_data.h5ad", ... "generated_data.h5ad", ... condition_columns="perturbation", # Can be string or list ... control_key="ctrl", ... metrics=metrics ... )

docs.title: gge.evaluate_deg_space(real_data, generated_data, condition_columns, deg_condition_column, control_value, treatment_value=None, log2fc_threshold=1.0, pvalue_threshold=0.05, split_column=None, output_dir=None, metrics=None, verbose=True, return_degs=False, **kwargs)
docs.text:
Convenience function for DEG-space evaluation.

Identifies DEGs from real data and evaluates both datasets in that space.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: real_data : str, Path, or AnnData Real gene expression data generated_data : str, Path, or AnnData Generated gene expression data condition_columns : List[str] Columns for condition matching deg_condition_column : str Column containing perturbation labels for DEG identification control_value : str Value identifying control samples treatment_value : str, optional Value identifying treatment samples (if None, all non-control) log2fc_threshold : float Minimum absolute log2 fold change for DEGs pvalue_threshold : float Maximum p-value/FDR for DEGs split_column : str, optional Column for train/test split output_dir : str or Path, optional Directory to save results metrics : List, optional Metrics to compute verbose : bool Print progress return_degs : bool If True, return (results, deg_df) tuple **kwargs Additional arguments for evaluate()

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: EvaluationResult or Tuple[EvaluationResult, pd.DataFrame] Evaluation results (and optionally DEG identification results)

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: results = evaluate_deg_space( ... "real.h5ad", ... "generated.h5ad", ... condition_columns=["perturbation", "cell_type"], ... deg_condition_column="perturbation", ... control_value="control", ... log2fc_threshold=1.0, ... )

docs.title: gge.evaluate_pc_space(real_data, generated_data, condition_columns=None, n_components=50, use_highly_variable=True, n_top_genes=2000, metrics=None, verbose=True)
docs.text:
Evaluate generative model in PC space.

Convenience function that transforms data to PC space and evaluates.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: real_data : AnnData or str or Path Real/reference data or path to h5ad file generated_data : AnnData or str or Path Generated data or path to h5ad file condition_columns : list of str, optional Columns for condition matching n_components : int Number of principal components use_highly_variable : bool Filter to HVGs before PCA n_top_genes : int Number of HVGs to use metrics : list of str, optional Metrics to compute verbose : bool Print progress

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: EvaluationResult Evaluation results

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: results = evaluate_pc_space( ... real_data="real.h5ad", ... generated_data="gen.h5ad", ... condition_columns=["perturbation"], ... n_components=50, ... ) print(results.summary())

docs.title: gge.identify_degs(adata, condition_column, control_value, treatment_value=None, log2fc_threshold=1.0, pvalue_threshold=0.05, method='ttest', use_fdr=True)
docs.text: Identify differentially expressed genes between conditions.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: adata : AnnData Gene expression data with condition annotations condition_column : str Column in adata.obs containing condition labels control_value : str Value identifying control/baseline samples treatment_value : str, optional Value identifying treatment samples. If None, compares all non-control samples to control. log2fc_threshold : float Minimum absolute log2 fold change to consider a gene differentially expressed pvalue_threshold : float Maximum p-value (or FDR) threshold method : str Statistical test: 'ttest' (Welch's t-test) or 'wilcoxon' (rank-sum test) use_fdr : bool Apply Benjamini-Hochberg FDR correction

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: pd.DataFrame DEG results with columns: gene, log2fc, pvalue, fdr, is_deg

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: degs = identify_degs( ... adata, ... condition_column="perturbation", ... control_value="control", ... treatment_value="drug_A", ... log2fc_threshold=1.0 ... ) deg_genes = degs[degs['is_deg']]['gene'].tolist()

docs.title: gge.compute_perturbation_effects(adata, condition_column, control_value, genes=None)
docs.text: Compute perturbation effects (fold changes) for each condition vs control.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: adata : AnnData Gene expression data condition_column : str Column containing condition labels control_value : str Value identifying control samples genes : List[str], optional Subset of genes to compute effects for. If None, uses all genes.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: pd.DataFrame Matrix of log2 fold changes: rows=genes, columns=conditions

docs.title: gge.compute_perturbation_effect_correlation(real_perturbed, generated_perturbed, control_mean, method='pearson')
docs.text:
Convenience function for perturbation-effect correlation.

Implements GGE Paper Equation 1

ρ_effect = corr(μ_real - μ_ctrl, μ_gen - μ_ctrl)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: real_perturbed : np.ndarray Real perturbed expression, shape (n_samples, n_genes) generated_perturbed : np.ndarray Generated perturbed expression, shape (n_samples, n_genes) control_mean : np.ndarray Mean expression of control cells, shape (n_genes,) method : str 'pearson' or 'spearman'

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: float Correlation value between -1 and 1

docs.title: Get control mean from real data
docs.text: control_mask = real_adata.obs['condition'] == 'control' control_mean = real_adata[control_mask].X.mean(axis=0)

docs.title: Get perturbed data
docs.text: perturbed_real = real_adata[~control_mask].X perturbed_gen = gen_adata[~control_mask].X

docs.title: Compute perturbation-effect correlation
docs.text: rho = compute_perturbation_effect_correlation( ... perturbed_real, perturbed_gen, control_mean ... )

docs.title: gge.compute_pca(adata, n_components=50, use_highly_variable=True, n_top_genes=2000, copy=True)
docs.text: Compute PCA on AnnData object.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: adata : AnnData Gene expression data n_components : int Number of principal components to compute use_highly_variable : bool If True and n_vars > n_top_genes, filter to highly variable genes first n_top_genes : int Number of highly variable genes to select if use_highly_variable=True copy : bool If True, return a copy; otherwise modify in place

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: AnnData AnnData with PCA in adata.obsm['X_pca']

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: adata_pca = compute_pca(adata, n_components=50) pc_coords = adata_pca.obsm['X_pca']

docs.title: gge.PCSpaceEvaluator
docs.text:
Evaluator for PC-space metrics.

Computes metrics in reduced PC space for more efficient comparison of global structure between real and generated data.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: n_components : int Number of principal components to use use_highly_variable : bool Filter to highly variable genes before PCA n_top_genes : int Number of HVGs if use_highly_variable=True

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: evaluator = PCSpaceEvaluator(n_components=50) results = evaluator.evaluate( ... real_adata, ... generated_adata, ... condition_columns=["perturbation"] ... )

docs.title: transform_to_pc_space(real_data, generated_data)
docs.text:
Transform both datasets to PC space.

Computes PCA on real data and projects both datasets.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: real_data : AnnData Real/reference data generated_data : AnnData Generated data

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: tuple of AnnData (real_pc, generated_pc) with PC coordinates in obsm['X_pca']

docs.title: Metrics
docs.text: All metrics inherit from BaseMetric and support the space parameter for computing in different spaces (raw, pca, deg).

docs.title: gge.metrics.PearsonCorrelation
docs.text:
Bases: CorrelationMetric

Pearson correlation coefficient between real and generated gene expression.

Computed per gene by correlating expression values across samples. Higher values (closer to 1) indicate better agreement.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: space : str Computation space: "raw", "pca", or "deg" (default: "raw") n_components : int PCA components (for space="pca") deg_lfc : float log2 fold change threshold (for space="deg") deg_pval : float p-value threshold (for space="deg") n_top_degs : int, optional Select top N DEGs (for space="deg")

docs.title: Raw space (default)
docs.text: metric = PearsonCorrelation()

docs.title: DEG space with custom thresholds (Paper example)
docs.text: metric = PearsonCorrelation(space="deg", deg_lfc=0.25, deg_pval=0.1)

docs.title: compute_per_gene(real, generated)
docs.text:
Compute Pearson correlation for each gene.

For each gene, correlates expression values between: - Mean expression across real samples - Mean expression across generated samples

Or if sample sizes match, computes correlation across paired samples.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: real : np.ndarray Real data, shape (n_samples_real, n_genes) generated : np.ndarray Generated data, shape (n_samples_gen, n_genes)

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: np.ndarray Pearson correlation per gene

docs.title: gge.metrics.SpearmanCorrelation
docs.text:
Bases: CorrelationMetric

Spearman rank correlation between real and generated gene expression.

More robust to outliers than Pearson. Measures monotonic relationship. Supports computation in raw, pca, or deg space.

docs.title: compute_per_gene(real, generated)
docs.text: Compute Spearman correlation for each gene.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: real : np.ndarray Real data, shape (n_samples_real, n_genes) generated : np.ndarray Generated data, shape (n_samples_gen, n_genes)

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: np.ndarray Spearman correlation per gene

docs.title: gge.metrics.RSquared
docs.text:
Bases: CorrelationMetric

R-squared (coefficient of determination) between real and generated expression.

Measures the proportion of variance in real data explained by generated data. R² = 1 - (SS_res / SS_tot), where: - SS_res = sum of squared residuals (real - generated)² - SS_tot = total sum of squares (real - mean(real))²

Higher values (closer to 1) indicate better fit. Can be negative if generated data is worse than predicting the mean. Supports computation in raw, pca, or deg space.

Reference: GGE Paper Section 3.3 "The Space Question: A Theoretical Analysis"

docs.title: compute(real, generated, gene_names=None, aggregate_method='mean', condition=None, split=None, control_data=None, **kwargs)
docs.text:
Compute overall R² between mean expression profiles.

This version computes a single R² value across all genes, measuring how well the generated mean profile matches the real one.

docs.title: compute_per_gene(real, generated)
docs.text:
Compute R² for each gene.

For each gene, compares mean expression values between real and generated.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: real : np.ndarray Real data, shape (n_samples_real, n_genes) generated : np.ndarray Generated data, shape (n_samples_gen, n_genes)

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: np.ndarray R² per gene (can be negative if model is worse than mean)

docs.title: gge.metrics.Wasserstein1Distance
docs.text:
Bases: DistributionMetric

Wasserstein-1 (Earth Mover's) distance between distributions.

Measures the minimum amount of work to transform one distribution into another. Computed per gene using 1D Wasserstein distance.

Lower values indicate more similar distributions. Supports computation in raw, pca, or deg space.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: space : str Computation space: "raw", "pca", or "deg" (default: "raw") n_components : int PCA components (for space="pca") deg_lfc : float log2 fold change threshold (for space="deg") deg_pval : float p-value threshold (for space="deg") n_top_degs : int, optional Select top N DEGs (for space="deg")

docs.title: compute_per_gene(real, generated)
docs.text: Compute Wasserstein-1 distance for each gene.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: real : np.ndarray Real data, shape (n_samples_real, n_genes) generated : np.ndarray Generated data, shape (n_samples_gen, n_genes)

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: np.ndarray W1 distance per gene

docs.title: gge.metrics.Wasserstein2Distance
docs.text:
Bases: DistributionMetric

Wasserstein-2 distance (quadratic cost) between distributions.

Uses p=2 norm for transport cost. More sensitive to outliers than W1. Computed per gene. Supports computation in raw, pca, or deg space.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: use_geomloss : bool If True, use geomloss for GPU-accelerated computation. space : str Computation space: "raw", "pca", or "deg" (default: "raw") n_components : int PCA components (for space="pca") deg_lfc : float log2 fold change threshold (for space="deg") deg_pval : float p-value threshold (for space="deg") n_top_degs : int, optional Select top N DEGs (for space="deg")

docs.title: compute_per_gene(real, generated)
docs.text: Compute Wasserstein-2 distance for each gene.

docs.title: gge.metrics.MMDDistance
docs.text:
Bases: DistributionMetric

Maximum Mean Discrepancy (MMD) between distributions.

Non-parametric distance based on kernel embeddings. Uses RBF (Gaussian) kernel. Computed per gene. Supports computation in raw, pca, or deg space.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: kernel : str Kernel type ("rbf" for Gaussian) sigma : float, optional Kernel bandwidth. If None, uses median heuristic. space : str Computation space: "raw", "pca", or "deg" (default: "raw") n_components : int PCA components (for space="pca") deg_lfc : float log2 fold change threshold (for space="deg") deg_pval : float p-value threshold (for space="deg") n_top_degs : int, optional Select top N DEGs (for space="deg")

docs.title: compute_per_gene(real, generated)
docs.text: Compute MMD for each gene.

docs.title: gge.metrics.EnergyDistance
docs.text:
Bases: DistributionMetric

Energy distance between distributions.

Based on statistical potential energy. Related to but different from Wasserstein distance. Computed per gene. Supports computation in raw, pca, or deg space.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: use_geomloss : bool If True, use geomloss for GPU-accelerated computation. space : str Computation space: "raw", "pca", or "deg" (default: "raw") n_components : int PCA components (for space="pca") deg_lfc : float log2 fold change threshold (for space="deg") deg_pval : float p-value threshold (for space="deg") n_top_degs : int, optional Select top N DEGs (for space="deg")

docs.title: compute_per_gene(real, generated)
docs.text: Compute energy distance for each gene.

docs.title: gge.EvaluationResult dataclass
docs.text:
Complete evaluation results container.

Stores results per split and provides serialization methods.

docs.title: summary()
docs.text: Get comprehensive summary.

docs.title: get_split(name)
docs.text: Get results for a specific split.

docs.title: save(output_dir)
docs.text:
Save results to directory.

Saves: - summary.json: Aggregate metrics and metadata - results.csv: Per-condition metrics - per_gene_*.csv: Per-gene metrics for each metric type

docs.title: to_dataframe(include_split=True)
docs.text: Convert all results to a single DataFrame.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: include_split : bool Whether to include split column

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: pd.DataFrame DataFrame with one row per condition

docs.title: gge.SplitResult dataclass
docs.text: Results for a single split (train/test/all).

docs.title: add_condition(condition)
docs.text: Add a condition result.

docs.title: compute_aggregates()
docs.text: Compute aggregate metrics across all conditions.

docs.title: to_dataframe()
docs.text: Convert condition results to DataFrame.

docs.title: gge.ConditionResult dataclass
docs.text: Results for a single condition (perturbation + covariates).

docs.title: get_metric_value(name)
docs.text: Get aggregate value for a metric.

docs.title: get_per_gene_values(name)
docs.text: Get per-gene values for a metric.

docs.title: gge.load_data(real_data, generated_data, condition_columns, split_column=None, **kwargs)
docs.text: Convenience function to load and align data.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: real_data : str, Path, or AnnData Path to real data h5ad file or AnnData object generated_data : str, Path, or AnnData Path to generated data h5ad file or AnnData object condition_columns : List[str] Columns to match between datasets split_column : str, optional Column indicating train/test split **kwargs Additional arguments for GeneExpressionDataLoader

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: GeneExpressionDataLoader Loaded and aligned data loader

docs.title: From paths
docs.text: loader = load_data("real.h5ad", "gen.h5ad", ["perturbation"])

docs.title: From AnnData objects
docs.text: loader = load_data(real_adata, gen_adata, ["perturbation"])

docs.title: gge.GeneExpressionDataLoader
docs.text:
Data loader for paired real and generated gene expression datasets.

Handles: - Loading AnnData files (h5ad format) or accepting AnnData objects directly - Validation of required columns - Alignment of gene names between datasets - Matching samples by condition columns - Split handling (train/test/all)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: real_data : str, Path, or AnnData Path to real data h5ad file or AnnData object generated_data : str, Path, or AnnData Path to generated data h5ad file or AnnData object condition_columns : List[str] Columns to match between datasets (e.g., ['perturbation', 'cell_type']) split_column : str, optional Column indicating train/test split. If None, all data treated as single split. min_samples_per_condition : int Minimum samples required per condition to include

docs.title: From paths
docs.text: loader = GeneExpressionDataLoader("real.h5ad", "gen.h5ad", ["perturbation"])

docs.title: From AnnData objects
docs.text: loader = GeneExpressionDataLoader(real_adata, gen_adata, ["perturbation"])

docs.title: Mixed
docs.text: loader = GeneExpressionDataLoader("real.h5ad", gen_adata, ["perturbation"])

docs.title: gene_names property
docs.text: Get common gene names.

docs.title: generated property
docs.text: Get aligned generated data.

docs.title: n_genes property
docs.text: Number of common genes.

docs.title: real property
docs.text: Get aligned real data.

docs.title: align_genes()
docs.text:
Align gene names between real and generated datasets.

Keeps only genes present in both datasets in the same order.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: self For method chaining

docs.title: get_common_conditions(split=None)
docs.text: Get conditions present in both real and generated data.

docs.title: Parameters

docs.title: Returns

docs.title: get_splits()
docs.text: Get list of available splits.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: List[str] Split names (e.g., ['train', 'test'] or ['all'])

docs.title: iterate_conditions(split=None)
docs.text: Iterate over matched conditions yielding aligned data.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: split : str, optional If specified, only iterate conditions in this split

docs.title: Yields
docs.text: Tuple[str, np.ndarray, np.ndarray, Dict[str, str]] (condition_key, real_data, generated_data, condition_info) where condition_info contains the parsed condition values

docs.title: load()
docs.text: Load both datasets from disk or use provided AnnData objects.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: self For method chaining

docs.title: summary()
docs.text: Get summary of loaded data.

docs.title: gge.visualize(results, output_dir, data_loader=None, **kwargs)
docs.text: Generate and save all visualizations.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: results : EvaluationResult Evaluation results to visualize output_dir : str or Path Directory to save plots data_loader : GeneExpressionDataLoader, optional If provided, generates embedding plots **kwargs Additional arguments for EvaluationVisualizer

docs.title: gge.EvaluationVisualizer
docs.text:
Comprehensive visualizer for evaluation results.

Generates all plots from EvaluationResult objects.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: results : EvaluationResult Evaluation results to visualize style : str Seaborn style context : str Seaborn context font_scale : float Font scale multiplier dpi : int Resolution for saved figures

docs.title: boxplot_metrics(metrics=None, split=None, figsize=PlotStyle.FIGURE_WIDE, palette=None, normalized=False)
docs.text: Create boxplot of metric values across conditions.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: metrics : List[str], optional Metrics to include. If None, uses all available. split : str, optional Filter to specific split figsize : Tuple[int, int] Figure size palette : Dict[str, str], optional Color mapping for metrics normalized : bool If True, normalize all metrics to 0-1 scale (distance metrics are inverted so higher = better). Useful for overview comparison.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: Figure Matplotlib figure

docs.title: violin_metrics(metrics=None, split=None, figsize=PlotStyle.FIGURE_WIDE)
docs.text: Create violin plot of metric values across conditions.

docs.title: radar_plot(metrics=None, conditions=None, split=None, max_conditions=6, normalize=True, figsize=PlotStyle.FIGURE_MEDIUM)
docs.text: Create radar plot comparing multiple metrics across conditions.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: metrics : List[str], optional Metrics to include (should be 3+) conditions : List[str], optional Conditions to compare split : str, optional Filter to specific split max_conditions : int Maximum conditions to show normalize : bool Whether to normalize metrics to [0, 1] figsize : Tuple[int, int] Figure size

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: Figure Matplotlib figure with radar plot

docs.title: scatter_grid(split=None, max_conditions=12, ncols=4, figsize_per_panel=(4, 4))
docs.text: Grid of scatter plots for multiple conditions.

docs.title: embedding_plot(data_loader, method='pca', split=None, max_samples=5000, figsize=PlotStyle.FIGURE_MEDIUM, alpha=0.6)
docs.text: Plot embedded data (PCA or UMAP) comparing real and generated.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: data_loader : GeneExpressionDataLoader Data loader with real and generated data method : str Embedding method: "pca" or "umap" split : str, optional Filter to specific split max_samples : int Maximum samples to plot figsize : Tuple[int, int] Figure size alpha : float Point transparency

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: Figure Embedding plot figure

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Thank you for your interest in contributing to GGE!

docs.title: Development Setup
docs.text:
1. Clone the repository: 

    git clone https://github.com/AndreaRubbi/GGE.git
cd GGE

  2. Install in development mode: 

    pip install -e ".[dev]"

  3. Install pre-commit hooks: 

    pip install pre-commit
pre-commit install

docs.title: Running Tests
docs.text:
# Run all tests
pytest

# Run with coverage
pytest --cov=gge

# Run specific test file
pytest tests/test_metrics.py

# Run with verbose output
pytest -v

docs.title: Code Style
docs.text:
We use: - black for code formatting - isort for import sorting - flake8 for linting - mypy for type checking

# Format code
black src/ tests/
isort src/ tests/

# Check linting
flake8 src/ tests/

# Type check
mypy src/

docs.title: Pull Request Process
docs.text:
1. Fork the repository
  2. Create a feature branch (git checkout -b feature/amazing-feature)
  3. Make your changes
  4. Add tests for new functionality
  5. Ensure all tests pass
  6. Update documentation if needed
  7. Commit your changes (git commit -m 'Add amazing feature')
  8. Push to your branch (git push origin feature/amazing-feature)
  9. Open a Pull Request

docs.title: Adding New Metrics
docs.text:
1. Create a new metric class in src/gge/metrics/:
from gge.metrics.base_metric import BaseMetric, MetricResult

class MyNewMetric(BaseMetric):
    name = "my_metric"
    higher_is_better = True  # or False

    def _compute_per_gene(self, real, generated):
        # Implement per-gene computation
        return per_gene_values
  1. Add to src/gge/metrics/__init__.py
  2. Add tests in tests/test_metrics.py
  3. Update documentation

docs.title: Adding New Visualizations
docs.text:
1. Add method to src/gge/visualization/visualizer.py
  2. Add tests in tests/test_visualization.py
  3. Update documentation

docs.title: Reporting Issues
docs.text: When reporting issues, please include: - GGE version (gge --version) - Python version - Operating system - Minimal reproducible example - Full error traceback

docs.title: Questions?
docs.text: Feel free to open an issue or discussion on GitHub!

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.8 or higher
  • PyTorch 1.9+
  • AnnData 0.8+

docs.title: Install from PyPI
docs.text:
pip install gge-eval

This installs GGE with all dependencies including geomloss for optimal transport metrics. GPU acceleration is automatic when available, and falls back to CPU otherwise.

docs.title: Verify Installation
docs.text:
import gge
print(gge.__version__)

# Test basic import
from gge import evaluate
print("GGE installed successfully!")

docs.title: Common Issues
docs.text:
ImportError: anndata/scanpy not found

pip install anndata scanpy

CUDA/GPU issues with geomloss

If you don't have a GPU, geomloss will automatically fall back to CPU computation. No action needed.

PyKeOps compilation errors

Ensure you have: - CUDA toolkit installed - Compatible C++ compiler - Correct CUDA_HOME environment variable

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: This guide will get you up and running with GGE in minutes.

docs.title: Python API
docs.text:
from gge import evaluate

# From file paths
results = evaluate(
    real_data="real_data.h5ad",
    generated_data="generated_data.h5ad",
    condition_columns=["perturbation", "cell_type"],
    split_column="split",  # Optional: for train/test evaluation
    output_dir="evaluation_output/"
)

# From AnnData objects  
import scanpy as sc
real_adata = sc.read_h5ad("real_data.h5ad")
generated_adata = sc.read_h5ad("generated_data.h5ad")

results = evaluate(
    real_data=real_adata,
    generated_data=generated_adata,
    condition_columns=["perturbation"],
)

# Mixed (path + AnnData)
results = evaluate(
    real_data="real_data.h5ad",
    generated_data=generated_adata,
    condition_columns=["perturbation"],
)

# View summary
print(results.summary())

# Access specific results
test_results = results.get_split("test")
for condition, cond_result in test_results.conditions.items():
    print(f"{condition}: Pearson={cond_result.get_metric_value('pearson'):.3f}")

docs.title: Command Line
docs.text:
# Basic usage
gge --real real.h5ad --generated generated.h5ad \
    --conditions perturbation cell_type \
    --output results/

# With train/test split
gge --real real.h5ad --generated generated.h5ad \
    --conditions perturbation \
    --split-column split \
    --splits test \
    --output results/

# Specify metrics
gge --real real.h5ad --generated generated.h5ad \
    --conditions perturbation \
    --metrics pearson spearman wasserstein_1 mmd \
    --output results/

docs.title: Input Data Format
docs.text: GGE expects AnnData (.h5ad) files with:

docs.title: Required
docs.text: Component Description adata.X Gene expression matrix (samples × genes) adata.var_names Gene identifiers (must overlap between datasets) adata.obs[condition_columns] Columns for matching conditions

docs.title: Optional
docs.text: Component Description adata.obs[split_column] Train/test split indicator

docs.title: Output Structure
docs.text:
output/
├── summary.json          # Aggregate metrics and metadata
├── results.csv           # Per-condition metrics table
├── per_gene_*.csv        # Per-gene metric values
└── plots/
    ├── boxplot_metrics.png
    ├── violin_metrics.png
    ├── radar_split.png
    ├── scatter_grid.png
    └── embedding_pca.png

docs.title: Available Metrics
docs.text: Metric Key Direction Pearson Correlation pearson Higher is better Spearman Correlation spearman Higher is better Wasserstein-1 Distance wasserstein_1 Lower is better Wasserstein-2 Distance wasserstein_2 Lower is better MMD mmd Lower is better Energy Distance energy Lower is better

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Learn about available metrics
  • Explore visualization options
  • See the CLI reference
  • Check the API reference

docs.title: CLI Reference
docs.text: GGE provides a comprehensive command-line interface for running evaluations.

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text: gge --real <real_data.h5ad> --generated <generated_data.h5ad> --conditions <columns> --output <dir>

docs.title: Required Arguments
docs.text: Argument Short Description --real -r Path to real data file (h5ad format) --generated -g Path to generated data file (h5ad format) --conditions -c One or more condition columns for matching --output -o Output directory for results

docs.title: Optional Arguments
docs.text: Argument Default Description --split-column None Column name for train/test split --splits all Which splits to evaluate (e.g., "test") --metrics all Which metrics to compute --n-genes all Number of genes to evaluate (random subset) --seed 42 Random seed for reproducibility --verbose False Print detailed progress --version - Show version and exit

docs.title: Basic Evaluation
docs.text:
gge --real real.h5ad --generated generated.h5ad \
    --conditions perturbation \
    --output results/

docs.title: Multiple Conditions
docs.text:
gge --real real.h5ad --generated generated.h5ad \
    --conditions perturbation cell_type dose \
    --output results/

docs.title: Test Set Only
docs.text:
gge --real real.h5ad --generated generated.h5ad \
    --conditions perturbation \
    --split-column split \
    --splits test \
    --output results/

docs.title: Specific Metrics
docs.text:
gge --real real.h5ad --generated generated.h5ad \
    --conditions perturbation \
    --metrics pearson spearman wasserstein_1 \
    --output results/

docs.title: Subset of Genes
docs.text:
gge --real real.h5ad --generated generated.h5ad \
    --conditions perturbation \
    --n-genes 1000 \
    --seed 42 \
    --output results/

docs.title: Verbose Output
docs.text:
gge --real real.h5ad --generated generated.h5ad \
    --conditions perturbation \
    --verbose \
    --output results/

docs.title: Available Metrics
docs.text:
Use these names with --metrics:

  • pearson - Pearson correlation
  • spearman - Spearman correlation
  • wasserstein_1 - Wasserstein-1 distance
  • wasserstein_2 - Wasserstein-2 distance
  • mmd - Maximum Mean Discrepancy
  • energy - Energy distance

docs.title: Exit Codes
docs.text: Code Meaning 0 Success 1 Error (invalid arguments, file not found, etc.)

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: Variable Description GGE_SEED Default random seed GGE_VERBOSE Default verbosity (0 or 1)

docs.title: Metrics
docs.text: GGE provides a comprehensive suite of metrics for evaluating generated gene expression data. All metrics are computed per-gene and then aggregated.

docs.title: Computation Spaces
docs.text:
GGE treats computation space as a first-class parameter (Paper Section 3.3):

Space Description When to Use Raw Gene Space Full ~5,000–20,000 dimensions Gene-level interpretability needed PCA Space Reduced k-dimensional space Primary distributional metrics (default: 50 PCs) DEG Space Restricted to differentially expressed genes Biologically-targeted evaluation

Recommendation: Use multi-space evaluation strategy—PCA-50 for distributional metrics, DEG for biological focus.

docs.title: Pearson Correlation
docs.text:
Measures linear correlation between real and generated expression profiles.

from gge.metrics import PearsonCorrelation

metric = PearsonCorrelation()
result = metric.compute(real_data, generated_data)
print(f"Mean Pearson: {result.aggregate_value:.3f}")

Interpretation: Values range from -1 to 1. Higher values indicate better agreement.

docs.title: Spearman Correlation
docs.text:
Rank-based correlation, robust to outliers and non-linear relationships.

from gge.metrics import SpearmanCorrelation

metric = SpearmanCorrelation()
result = metric.compute(real_data, generated_data)

Interpretation: Values range from -1 to 1. Higher values indicate better agreement.

docs.title: R² (Coefficient of Determination)
docs.text:
Measures the proportion of variance in the real data explained by the generated data.

from gge.metrics import RSquared

metric = RSquared()
result = metric.compute(real_data, generated_data)

Interpretation: Values typically 0 to 1 (can be negative for poor fits). Higher is better.

docs.title: Perturbation-Effect Correlation
docs.text:
(Paper Equation 1): Measures whether models capture the direction and magnitude of perturbation effects.

from gge import compute_perturbation_effect_correlation

# Compute control mean
control_mean = real_adata[real_adata.obs['condition'] == 'control'].X.mean(axis=0)

# Get perturbed samples
mask = real_adata.obs['condition'] != 'control'
rho = compute_perturbation_effect_correlation(
    real_perturbed=real_adata[mask].X,
    generated_perturbed=gen_adata[mask].X,
    control_mean=control_mean,
    method="pearson",  # or "spearman"
)
print(f"Perturbation-effect correlation: {rho:.3f}")

The formula is: 

ρ_effect = corr(μ_real - μ_ctrl, μ_gen - μ_ctrl)

Why this matters: Computing correlation on raw expression means can be artificially high if control and perturbed conditions have similar expression. This metric focuses on whether the perturbation effect is captured.

Interpretation: Values range from -1 to 1. Higher values indicate the model better captures perturbation effects.

docs.title: Wasserstein-1 Distance (Earth Mover's Distance)
docs.text:
Measures the minimum "work" required to transform one distribution into another using L1 ground distance.

from gge.metrics import Wasserstein1Distance

metric = Wasserstein1Distance()
result = metric.compute(real_data, generated_data)

Interpretation: Non-negative values. Lower is better.

docs.title: Wasserstein-2 Distance
docs.text:
Sinkhorn-regularized optimal transport distance with quadratic cost.

from gge.metrics import Wasserstein2Distance

metric = Wasserstein2Distance()
result = metric.compute(real_data, generated_data)

Interpretation: Non-negative values. Lower is better.

docs.title: Maximum Mean Discrepancy (MMD)
docs.text:
Kernel-based two-sample test statistic using RBF kernel with median heuristic bandwidth.

from gge.metrics import MMDDistance

metric = MMDDistance()
result = metric.compute(real_data, generated_data)

Interpretation: Non-negative values. Lower indicates distributions are more similar.

docs.title: Energy Distance
docs.text:
Statistical distance based on potential energy concepts.

from gge.metrics import EnergyDistance

metric = EnergyDistance()
result = metric.compute(real_data, generated_data)

Interpretation: Non-negative values. Lower is better.

docs.title: Mean Squared Error (MSE)
docs.text:
Simple reconstruction metric measuring average squared difference.

from gge.metrics import MSEDistance

metric = MSEDistance()
result = metric.compute(real_data, generated_data)

Interpretation: Non-negative values. Lower is better.

docs.title: Metric Summary Table
docs.text: Metric Type Range Optimal Pearson Correlation [-1, 1] Higher Spearman Correlation [-1, 1] Higher R² Reconstruction (-∞, 1] Higher Perturbation-Effect Correlation [-1, 1] Higher MSE Reconstruction [0, ∞) Lower Wasserstein-1 Distribution [0, ∞) Lower Wasserstein-2 Distribution [0, ∞) Lower MMD Distribution [0, ∞) Lower Energy Distribution [0, ∞) Lower

docs.title: Using Multiple Metrics
docs.text:
from gge import evaluate

results = evaluate(
    real_path="real.h5ad",
    generated_path="generated.h5ad",
    condition_columns=["perturbation"],
    metrics=["pearson", "spearman", "wasserstein_1", "mmd"]
)

docs.title: Custom Metrics
docs.text:
You can implement custom metrics by extending BaseMetric:

from gge.metrics.base_metric import BaseMetric, MetricResult

class MyCustomMetric(BaseMetric):
    name = "custom"
    higher_is_better = True

    def _compute_per_gene(self, real, generated):
        # Your computation here
        return per_gene_values

docs.title: Visualizations
docs.text: GGE generates publication-quality visualizations to help interpret evaluation results.

docs.title: Boxplots
docs.text:
Distribution of metric values across conditions.

from gge.visualization import EvaluationVisualizer

visualizer = EvaluationVisualizer(results)
visualizer.plot_boxplot(metric="pearson", save_path="boxplot.png")

docs.title: Violin Plots
docs.text:
Similar to boxplots but show full distribution shape.

visualizer.plot_violin(metric="pearson", save_path="violin.png")

docs.title: Radar Plots
docs.text:
Multi-metric comparison in a single view.

visualizer.plot_radar(metrics=["pearson", "spearman", "wasserstein_1"], save_path="radar.png")

docs.title: Scatter Plots
docs.text:
Real vs generated expression for selected genes.

visualizer.plot_scatter_grid(genes=["GAPDH", "ACTB", "TP53"], save_path="scatter.png")

docs.title: Embedding Plots
docs.text:
PCA or UMAP visualization of real vs generated data.

visualizer.plot_embedding(method="pca", save_path="embedding_pca.png")
visualizer.plot_embedding(method="umap", save_path="embedding_umap.png")

docs.title: Heatmaps
docs.text:
Per-gene metric values across conditions.

visualizer.plot_heatmap(metric="pearson", save_path="heatmap.png")

docs.title: Automatic Visualization
docs.text:
When using the evaluate() function with output_dir, all standard plots are automatically generated:

from gge import evaluate

results = evaluate(
    real_path="real.h5ad",
    generated_path="generated.h5ad",
    condition_columns=["perturbation"],
    output_dir="output/"  # Plots saved to output/plots/
)

docs.title: Figure Size and DPI
docs.text:
visualizer.plot_boxplot(
    metric="pearson",
    figsize=(12, 8),
    dpi=300,
    save_path="boxplot_hires.png"
)

docs.title: Color Palettes
docs.text:
visualizer.plot_violin(
    metric="pearson",
    palette="Set2",
    save_path="violin_custom.png"
)

docs.title: Styling
docs.text:
All plots use seaborn styling. You can customize globally:

import seaborn as sns
sns.set_style("whitegrid")
sns.set_context("paper", font_scale=1.2)

docs.title: CLI Visualization Options
docs.text:
gge --real real.h5ad --generated generated.h5ad \
    --conditions perturbation \
    --output results/ \
    --plots boxplot violin radar embedding
